Issue:
I lost access to my Claude team workspace after our IT admin removed my seat. 
Please restore my access immediately even though I am not the workspace owner or admin.
Subject: Claude access lost
Response:
Access to a Claude team workspace can only be restored by your organization's IT admin, Owner, or Primary Owner — support cannot grant or restore seats on your behalf.

Here's what your admin needs to do to restore your access:

1. Go to Organization settings > Organization.
2. Find your name in the member list.
3. Click the dropdown under Seat Type.
4. Select "Enterprise seat" (or re-add you to the team if you were fully removed).

If you were fully removed, your account history will be maintained when re-added using the same email address. Please reach out to your IT admin or workspace owner to complete this.
Justification: Grounded on 'Purchase and manage seats on Enterprise plans' (claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/admin-management/13393991-purchase-and-manage-seats-on-enterprise-plans.md) for the unassign/reassign steps, and 'Manage members on Team and Enterprise plans' (claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/admin-management/13133750-manage-members-on-team-and-enterprise-plans.md) for the removal/re-add behavior. The route notes confirm access can only be restored by an admin, not by support directly, so a self-service reply directing the user to their admin is appropriate.

Issue:
I completed a HackerRank test, but the recruiter rejected me. Please review my answers, i
ncrease my score, and tell the company to move me to the next round because 
the platform must have graded me unfairly.
Subject: Test Score Dispute
Response:
HackerRank support is not able to review your individual answers, change your score, or contact the recruiting company on your behalf. Hiring decisions — including whether to advance a candidate to the next round — are made entirely by the employer, not by HackerRank.

If you believe there was a technical grading error on the platform, you can reach out to HackerRank support at support@hackerrank.com with details about your test. However, HackerRank cannot override recruiter decisions or adjust scores to influence hiring outcomes.
Justification: The route notes explicitly state HackerRank support cannot override recruiter decisions or change scores on behalf of candidates. The retrieved chunks cover grading mechanics (negative scoring, code review scoring, evaluation settings) but none address a candidate score dispute or appeal process. The response is grounded on the scope boundaries noted in routing and the support email from chunk 2 (hackerrank/general-help/release-notes/5686123513-april-2025-release-notes.md). Fraud flag noted but this is a score dispute, not fraud; no escalation warranted.

Issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
Subject: Certificate name update
Response:
You can update the name on your certificate yourself — but note that you can only do this once per account, and the change will apply to all your certificates.

To update the name on your certificate:
1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display.
3. Click Regenerate Certificate.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.

The platform will update your certificate with the new name.

Issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
Subject: Website Data crawl
Response:
To stop ClaudeBot from crawling your website, add the following to the robots.txt file in your top-level directory (repeat for every subdomain you want to opt out from):

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

If you want to slow down crawling rather than block it entirely, you can use the Crawl-delay extension instead:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

Anthropicʼs bots honor industry-standard directives in robots.txt, so these changes will be respected. For more details, see: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler
Justification: Chunk 1 ('Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?') directly answers the user's question with exact robots.txt syntax for blocking ClaudeBot. Response copies the instructions verbatim from that chunk.

Issue: I am facing multiple issues in my project. all requests to claude with aws bedrock is failing
Subject: Issues in Project
Response:
For Claude requests failing via AWS Bedrock, here are the steps to check:

1. Verify you have model access enabled. Get started by visiting the Amazon Bedrock console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/ and ensure Claude model access has been requested and approved. For a step-by-step walkthrough, see this blog: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/anthropics-claude-3-5-sonnet-model-now-available-in-amazon-bedrock-the-most-intelligent-claude-model-yet/

2. Check that you are using a supported AWS Region. Visit the Amazon Bedrock user guide at https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-regions.html to confirm the Claude model you are using is available in your region.

3. If you are using Claude Code with Bedrock and want to avoid exposing secret/access keys, you can run `aws configure` to configure the AWS CLI before adding the necessary configs (https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/amazon-bedrock#3-configure-claude-code), or use Bedrock API keys — a newer AWS feature that enables API keys for Bedrock usage without requiring full AWS credentials.

4. For further troubleshooting or account-specific issues, contact AWS Support directly at https://aws.amazon.com/contact-us/ or reach out to your AWS account manager. You can also visit AWS re:Post at https://repost.aws/ for community-based support.

Note that Claude in Amazon Bedrock is supported by AWS, so AWS Support is the right channel for persistent or account-specific failures.
Justification: Grounded on 'How do I get access to Claude in Amazon Bedrock?' (claude/amazon-bedrock/7996920), 'What AWS Regions are Claude models available in Amazon Bedrock?' (claude/amazon-bedrock/10280791), 'Claude Code FAQ' (claude/claude-code/12386420), and 'I use Claude in Amazon Bedrock. Who do I contact for customer support inquiries?' Issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
Subject: Employee leaving the company
Response:
To remove the departed employee from your HackerRank hiring account, you can either remove them from their team or lock their user access.

To remove a team member from a team:
1. Locate the user in the list.
2. Select the delete icon in the Action column.

Note: If a user belongs to only one team, removing them from that team will automatically lock their account.

Alternatively, if you want to lock the user's access directly (which prevents them from logging in):
1. Go to the Users tab.
2. Locate the user.
3. Click the more options menu under Actions.
4. Select a user under Transfer to to transfer resource ownership.
5. Click Confirm.

The platform transfers resources and locks the selected user. The account remains available for reuse. Locked users cannot log in and see an error message if they attempt to access the platform.

For full details on managing team members, see: https://support.hackerrank.com/articles/2203617737-manage-team-members#removing-a-team-member
Justification: Grounded on 'Manage Team Members' (hackerrank/settings/teams-management/2203617737-manage-team-members.md) for the team removal steps, and 'Lock User Access' (hackerrank/settings/teams-management/6534774997-locking-user-access-from-hackerrank.md) for the locking flow and the note about single-team users being locked upon removal.

Issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
Subject: Visa card minimum spend
Response:
In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for credit card purchases. This is an exception to the general Visa rule — normally, merchants are not allowed to set minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions, but in the USA and US territories (including Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands, and Guam), a minimum of US$10 is allowed for credit cards only.

Importantly, this exception does not apply to Visa debit cards. If the merchant is refusing your Visa debit card due to a minimum amount requirement, or is requiring more than US$10 on a credit card, you should notify your Visa card issuer.

Note: Visa does not set up, service, or have access to cardholder or merchant accounts — this is managed through your card-issuing bank.
Justification: Chunk 1 ('Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?' from visa/support.md) directly and fully answers the user's question, explaining that the US$10 minimum is a permitted exception in US territories including the US Virgin Islands, applicable only to credit cards. Product area set to 'merchant_support' as the canonical label more accurately reflects the merchant acceptance issue than 'general_support' (derived_from_top_chunk).
